book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.

That it was before predicted that the world would hold us in abhorrence, and that it would stir up persecutions against us, and that no new thing is happening to the Christians, since from the beginning of the world the good have suffered, and the righteous have been oppressed and slain by the unrighteous.

ccel.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

title: That it was before predicted that the world would hold us in abhorrence, and that it would stir up persecutions against us, and that no new thing is happening to the Christians, since from the beginning of the world the good have suffered, and the righteous have been oppressed and slain by the unrighteous.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That it was before predicted that the world would hold us in abhorrence, and that it would stir up persecutions against us, and that no new thing is happening to the Christians, since from the beginning of the world the good have suffered, and the righteous have been oppressed and slain by the unrighteous.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The Lord in the Gospel forewarns and foretells, saying: “If the world hates you, know that it first hated me.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If ye were of the world, the world would love what is its own: but because ye are not of the world, and I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Remember the word that I spoke unto you, The servant is not greater than his master.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If they have persecuted me, they will persecute you also.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “The hour will come, that every one that killeth you will think that he doeth God service; but they will do this because they have not known the Father nor me.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But these things have I told you, that when the hour shall come ye may remember them, because I told you.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice; ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “These things have I spoken unto you, that in me ye may have peace; but in the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good confidence, for I have overcome the world.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And when He was interrogated by His disciples concerning the sign of His coming, and of the consummation of the world, He answered and said: “Take care lest any deceive you: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And ye shall begin to hear of wars, and rumours of wars; see that ye be not troubled: for these things must needs come to pass, but the end is not yet.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and earthquakes, and pestilences, in every place.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But all these things are the beginnings of travailings.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Then they shall deliver you up into affliction, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hateful to all nations for my name’s sake.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And many false prophets shall arise, and shall seduce many; and because wickedness shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But he who shall endure to the end, the same shall be saved.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And this Gospel of the kingdom shall be preached through all the world, for a testimony to all nations; and then shall come the end.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: When, therefore, ye shall see the abomination of desolation which is spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let him who readeth understand), then let them which are in Judea flee to the mountains; and let him which is on the house-roof not go down to take anything from the house; and let him who is in the field not return back to carry away his clothes.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But woe to them that are pregnant, and to those that are giving suck in those days!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, nor on the Sabbath-day: for there shall be great tribulation, such as has not arisen from the beginning of the world until now, neither shall arise.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And unless those days should be shortened, no flesh should be saved; but for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Then if any one shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or, Lo, there; believe him not.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall show great signs and wonders, to cause error, if it be possible, even to the elect.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If, therefore, they shall say to you, Lo, he is in the desert; go not forth: lo, he is in the sleeping chambers; believe it not.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For as the flashing of lightning goeth forth from the east, and appeareth even to the west, so also shall the coming of the Son of man be.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Wheresoever the carcase shall be, there shall the eagles be gathered together.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But immediately after the affliction of those days the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of heaven shall be moved: and then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and all the tribes of the earth shall lament, and shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with great power and glory.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And He shall send His angels with a great trumpet, and they shall gather together His elect from the four winds, from the heights of heaven, even into the farthest bounds thereof.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And these are not new or sudden things which are now happening to Christians; since the good and righteous, and those who are devoted to God in the law of innocence and the fear of true religion, advance always through afflictions, and wrongs, and the severe and manifold penalties of troubles, in the hardship of a narrow path.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Thus, at the very beginning of the world, the righteous Abel was the first to be slain by his brother; and Jacob was driven into exile, and Joseph was sold, and king Saul persecuted the merciful David; and king Ahab endeavoured to oppress Elias, who firmly and bravely asserted the majesty of God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Zacharias the priest was slain between the temple and the altar, that himself might there become a sacrifice where he was accustomed to offer sacrifices to God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: So many martyrdoms of the righteous have, in fact, often been celebrated; so many examples of faith and virtue have been set forth to future generations.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The three youths, Ananias, Azarias, and Misäel, equal in age, agreeing in love, stedfast in faith, constant in virtue, stronger than the flames and penalties that urged them, proclaim that they only obey God, that they know Him alone, that they worship Him alone, saying: “O king Nebuchodonosor, there is no need for us to answer thee in this matter.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For the God whom we serve is able to deliver us out of the furnace of burning fire; and He will deliver us from thy hands, O king.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And if not, be it known unto thee, that we do not serve thy gods, and we do not adore the golden image which thou hast set up.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And Daniel, devoted to God, and filled with the Holy Spirit, exclaims and says: “I worship nothing but the Lord my God, who founded the heaven and the earth.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Tobias also, although under a royal and tyrannical slavery, yet in feeling and spirit free, maintains his confession to God, and sublimely announces both the divine power and majesty, saying: “In the land of my captivity I confess to Him, and I show forth His power in a sinful nation.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: What, indeed, do we find in the Maccabees of seven brethren, equals alike in their lot of birth and virtues, filling up the number seven in the sacrament of a perfected completion?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Seven brethren were thus associating in martyrdom.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: As the first seven days in the divine arrangement containing seven thousand of years, as the seven spirits and seven angels which stand and go in and out before the face of God, and the seven-branched lamp in the tabernacle of witness, and the seven golden candlesticks in the Apocalypse, and the seven columns in Solomon upon which Wisdom built her house; so here also the number seven of the brethren, embracing, in the quantity of their number, the seven churches, as likewise in the first book of Kings we read that the barren hath borne seven.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And in Isaiah seven women lay hold on one man, whose name they ask to be called upon them.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And the Apostle Paul, who refers to this lawful and certain number, writes to the seven churches.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And in the Apocalypse the Lord directs His divine and heavenly precepts to the seven churches and their angels, which number is now found in this case, in the seven brethren, that a lawful consummation may be completed.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: With the seven children is manifestly associated also the mother, their origin and root, who subsequently begat seven churches, she herself having been first, and alone founded upon a rock by the voice of the Lord.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Nor is it of no account that in their sufferings the mother alone is with her children.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For martyrs who witness themselves as the sons of God in suffering are now no more counted as of any father but God, as in the Gospel the Lord teaches, saying, “Call no man your father upon earth; for one is your Father, which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But what utterances of confessions did they herald forth! how illustrious, how great proofs of faith did they afford!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The king Antiochus, their enemy—yea, in Antiochus Antichrist was set forth—sought to pollute the mouths of martyrs, glorious and unconquered in the spirit of confession, with the contagion of swine’s flesh; and when he had severely beaten them with whips, and could prevail nothing, commanded iron plates to be heated, which being heated and made to glow, he commanded him who had first spoken, and had more provoked the king with the constancy of his virtue and faith, to be brought up and roasted, his tongue having first been pulled out and cut off, which had confessed God; and this happened the more gloriously to the martyr.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For the tongue which had confessed the name of God, ought itself first to go to God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Then in the second, sharper pains having been devised, before he tortured the other limbs, he tore off the skin of his head with the hair, doubtless with a purpose in his hatred.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For since Christ is the head of the man, and God is the head of Christ, he who tore the head in the martyr was persecuting God and Christ in that head.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But he, trusting in his martyrdom, and promising to himself from the retribution of God the reward of resurrection, exclaimed and said, “Thou indeed impotently destroyest us out of this present life; but the King of the world will raise us up, who die for His laws, unto the eternal resurrection of life.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The third being challenged, quickly put forth his tongue; for he had learned from his brother to despise the punishment of cutting off the tongue.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Moreover, he firmly held forth his hands to be cut off, greatly happy in such a mode of punishment, since it was his lot to imitate, by stretching forth his hands, the form of his Lord’s passion.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And also the fourth, with like virtue, despising the tortures, and answering, to restrain the king, with a heavenly voice exclaimed, and said, “It is better that those who are given to death by men should wait for hope from God, to be raised up by Him again to eternal life.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For to thee there shall be no resurrection to life.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The fifth, besides treading under foot the torments of the king, and his severe and various tortures, by the strength of faith, animated to prescience also and knowledge of future events by the Spirit of divinity, foretold to the king the wrath of God, and the vengeance that should swiftly follow.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: “Having power,” said he, “among men, though thou art corruptible, thou doest what thou wilt.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But think not that our race is forsaken of God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Abide, and see His great power, how He will torment thee and thy seed.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: What alleviation was that to the martyr! how substantial a comfort in his sufferings, not to consider his own torments, but to predict the penalties of his tormentor!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But in the sixth, not his bravery only, but also his humility, is to be set forth; that the martyr claimed nothing to himself, nor even made an account of the honour of his own confession with proud words, but rather ascribed it to his sins that he was suffering persecution from the king, while he attributed to God that afterwards he should be avenged.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: He taught that martyrs are modest, that they were confident of vengeance, and boasted nothing in their suffering.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: “Do not,” said he, “needlessly err; for we on our own account suffer these things, as sinning against our God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But think not thou that thou shalt be unpunished, who darest to fight against God.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Also the admirable mother, who, neither broken down by the weakness of her sex, nor moved by her manifold bereavement, looked upon her dying children with cheerfulness, and did not reckon those things punishments of her darlings, but glories, giving as great a witness to God by the virtue of her eyes, as her children had given by the tortures and suffering of their limbs; when, after the punishment and slaying of six, there remained one of the brethren, to whom the king promised riches, and power, and many things, that his cruelty and ferocity might be soothed by the satisfaction of even one being subdued, and asked that the mother would entreat that her son might be cast down with herself; she entreated, but it was as became a mother of martyrs—as became one who was mindful of the law and of God—as became one who loved her sons not delicately, but bravely.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For she entreated, but it was that he would confess God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: She entreated that the brother would not be separated from his brothers in the alliance of praise and glory; then only considering herself the mother of seven sons, if it should happen to her to have brought forth seven sons, not to the world, but to God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Therefore arming him, and strengthening him, and so bearing her son by a more blessed birth, she said, “O son, pity me that bare thee ten months in the womb, and gave thee milk for three years, and nourished thee and brought thee up to this age; I pray thee, O son, look upon the heaven and the earth; and having considered all the things which are in them, understand that out of nothing God made these things and the human race.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Therefore, O son, do not fear that executioner; but being made worthy of thy brethren, receive death, that in the same mercy I may receive thee with thy brethren.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: The mother’s praise was great in her exhortation to virtue, but greater in the fear of God and in the truth of faith, that she promised nothing to herself or her son from the honour of the six martyrs, nor believed that the prayer of the brothers would avail for the salvation of one who should deny, but rather persuaded him to become a sharer in their suffering, that in the day of judgment he might be found with his brethren.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: After this the mother also dies with her children; for neither was anything else becoming, than that she who had borne and made martyrs, should be joined in the fellowship of glory with them, and that she herself should follow those whom she had sent before to God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And lest any, when the opportunity either of a certificate or of any such matter is offered to him whereby he may deceive, should embrace the wicked part of deceivers, let us not be silent, moreover, about Eleazar, who, when an opportunity was offered him by the ministers of the king, that having received the flesh which it was allowable for him to partake of, he might pretend, for the misguiding of the king, that he ate those things which were forced upon him from the sacrifices and unlawful meats, would not consent to this deception, saying that it was fitting neither for his age nor nobility to feign that, whereby others would be scandalized and led into error; if they should think that Eleazar, being ninety years old, had left and betrayed the law of God, and had gone over to the manner of aliens; and that it was not of so much consequence to gain the short moments of life, and so incur eternal punishment from an offended God.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And he having been long tortured, and now at length reduced to extremity, while he was dying in the midst of stripes and tortures, groaned and said, “O Lord, that hast the holy knowledge, it is manifest that although I might be delivered from death, I suffer the severest pains of body, being beaten with scourges; but with my mind, on account of Thy fear, I willingly suffer these things.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Assuredly his faith was sincere and his virtue sound, and abundantly pure, not to have regarded king Antiochus, but God the Judge, and to have known that it could not avail him for salvation if he should mock and deceive man, when God, who is the judge of our conscience, and who only is to be feared, cannot at all be mocked nor deceived.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: If, therefore, we also live as dedicated and devoted to God—if we make our way over the ancient and sacred footsteps of the righteous, let us go through the same proofs of sufferings, the same testimonies of passions, considering the glory of our time the greater on this account, that while ancient examples may be numbered, yet that subsequently, when the abundance of virtue and faith was in excess, the Christian martyrs cannot be numbered, as the Apocalypse testifies and says: “After these things I beheld a great multitude, which no man could number, of every nation, and of every tribe, and people, and language, standing in the sight of the throne and of the Lamb; and they were clothed in white robes, and palms were in their hands; and they said with a loud voice, Salvation to our God, who sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb!

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And one of the elders answered and said unto me, Who are those which are arrayed in white robes, and whence come they?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And I said unto him, My lord, thou knowest.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And he said unto me, These are they who have come out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve Him day and night in His temple.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: But if the assembly of the Christian martyrs is shown and proved to be so great, let no one think it a hard or a difficult thing to become a martyr, when he sees that the crowd of martyrs cannot be numbered.
